skill.name: stripe-integration
skill.description: Implement Stripe payment processing for robust, PCI-compliant payment flows including checkout, subscriptions, and webhooks. Use when integrating Stripe payments, building subscription systems, or handling recurring billing.
skill.summary: Stripe payment integration skill for implementing PCI-compliant checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and refund handling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 47 static findings are false positives. The skill contains legitimate Stripe payment integration documentation with Python code examples. Detected patterns (backtick execution, hardcoded URLs, API key access) are benign documentation artifacts: backticks are markdown code fences, URLs are placeholder examples, and API key patterns are documented placeholders showing where users should insert their own keys.

medium_findings.title: External Command Detection in Code Blocks
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected Ruby/shell backtick execution patterns at 26 locations. Investigation reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES - the detections are backticks used for markdown code fencing in Python code examples (e.g., `import stripe`, `stripe.api_key = ...`). This is legitimate documentation formatting, not command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks are markdown code fences containing Python code samples for Stripe API integration. No shell or Ruby commands are executed.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Code Examples
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected hardcoded URLs. Investigation reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES - placeholder example URLs like 'https://yourdomain.com/success' in code samples showing users where to configure their own callback URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs like 'https://yourdomain.com/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}' are documented placeholders, not actual hardcoded production URLs.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Keys
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected API key access patterns at lines 81 and 394. Investigation reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES - these are documented placeholders 'sk_test_...' showing users where to insert their own test API keys, a standard documentation practice for Stripe integration guides.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern 'stripe.api_key = "sk_test_..."' is a documented placeholder showing where users should configure their own API key, not a leaked credential.

content.value_statement: This skill provides comprehensive Stripe payment integration guidance for implementing checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and refund handling with PCI-compliant best practices.
actual_capabilities: Create Stripe checkout sessions for one-time and recurring payments
Implement custom payment intents with full UI control
Handle Stripe webhooks securely with signature verification
Manage customer subscriptions and billing cycles
Process refunds and handle payment disputes
Implement Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) for European payments
limitations: Skill provides code examples and guidance only - does not execute payment processing itself
Does not include a pre-built payment UI component library
Does not provide backend server implementation - users must implement their own server
Does not include Stripe Connect marketplace functionality documentation

use_cases.title: E-commerce Checkout Implementation
use_cases.description: Add Stripe checkout to web applications for accepting one-time payments with hosted checkout pages.

use_cases.title: SaaS Subscription Management
use_cases.description: Set up recurring billing for SaaS products with customer portal access and subscription lifecycle management.

use_cases.title: Webhook Event Processing
use_cases.description: Process payment events securely with signature verification and idempotent handling for production systems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Checkout Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a basic Stripe checkout flow for a one-time payment of $29. Show me how to create a checkout session and redirect the user.

prompt_templates.title: Subscription Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a subscription checkout flow for a monthly service at $19/month. Include customer creation and subscription activation.

prompt_templates.title: Webhook Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a secure webhook handler in Python that verifies the Stripe signature and processes payment_intent.succeeded events.

prompt_templates.title: Refund Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to implement partial refunds and handle the refund workflow including the reason codes.

output_examples.input: Create a checkout session for a $49 product
output_examples.output:
I can help you create a Stripe checkout session. Here's the Python code:

```python
import stripe

stripe.api_key = 'sk_test_YOUR_KEY'

session = stripe.checkout.Session.create(
    payment_method_types=['card'],
    line_items=[{
        'price_data': {
            'currency': 'usd',
            'product_data': {'name': 'Premium Widget'},
            'unit_amount': 4900,  # $49.00 in cents
        },
        'quantity': 1,
    }],
    mode='payment',
    success_url='https://yoursite.com/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}',
    cancel_url='https://yoursite.com/cancel',
)

# Redirect user to session.url
```

This creates a hosted checkout page. The user will be redirected to Stripe's secure payment form.

output_examples.input: Handle a payment succeeded webhook securely

best_practices: Always verify webhook signatures using stripe.Webhook.construct_event to prevent spoofed events
Use Stripe's hosted checkout pages to minimize PCI compliance burden
Handle webhook events idempotently by checking for duplicate event IDs before processing
Store only payment method IDs, never raw card data, to maintain PCI compliance
Use test mode keys during development and verify all payment flows with test card numbers
anti_patterns: Do not rely on client-side payment confirmation alone - always verify via webhooks
Do not hardcode API keys in source code - use environment variables or secrets management
Do not process the same webhook event twice - implement idempotency checks
Do not use production API keys in test mode or test cards in production

faq.question: What is the difference between Checkout Sessions and Payment Intents?
faq.answer: Checkout Sessions use Stripe's hosted payment page for minimal implementation effort and PCI compliance. Payment Intents give you full control to build a custom payment UI within your app.

faq.question: How do I verify that webhooks actually come from Stripe?
faq.answer: Use stripe.Webhook.construct_event() with your endpoint secret to verify the Stripe-Signature header. This ensures the request genuinely came from Stripe.

faq.question: What are test card numbers I can use?
faq.answer: Use 4242424242424242 for successful payments, 4000000000000002 for declined cards, and 4000002500003155 for 3D Secure required cards.

faq.question: How do I handle Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) for European customers?
faq.answer: Stripe automatically handles SCA when you use Checkout Sessions or enable automatic_payment_methods. For custom flows, use Stripe Elements with the new authentication flows.

faq.question: Can I process partial refunds?
faq.answer: Yes, provide the amount parameter in cents when creating a refund. Without it, Stripe processes a full refund. You can issue multiple partial refunds up to the original payment amount.

faq.question: How do I give customers access to manage their subscriptions?
faq.answer: Use Stripe Billing Portal by calling stripe.billing_portal.Session.create() with the customer ID. This provides a Stripe-hosted page for customers to update payment methods, cancel, or pause subscriptions.
